name: nbstripout (Strip Notebook Output for Git)

performance.notes: Fast stripping; runs as git filter on commit

agent_readiness.notes: Strip notebook outputs before git commit. nbstripout --install → installs git filter for .ipynb files in current repo. nbstripout notebook.ipynb → strip output in place. nbstripout --status → show installed filters. nbstripout --uninstall → remove filters. git config --global filter.nbstripout.smudge cat (restore on checkout). .gitattributes: *.ipynb filter=nbstripout. Strips: output, execution_count, cell metadata. keep_count: nbstripout --keep-count (preserve execution_count). keep_output: nbstripout --keep-output. pre-commit: - repo: https://github.com/kynan/nbstripout hooks: - id: nbstripout. Prevents committing outputs (images, data, errors) to git. Essential for clean notebook diffs and avoiding large binary blobs.

gotchas: --install is per-repo (not global) by default — must install in each repo
Strips output locally on commit — colleagues need to run cells again
Large outputs committed before install remain in git history
Some teams want to preserve outputs — make this opt-in, not mandatory
best_when: Team notebooks where clean diffs are important
Avoiding large binary outputs (images, DataFrames) in git
Pre-commit hooks ensuring clean notebook commits
CI/CD repos where notebook output pollutes diffs
avoid_when: When notebook outputs are the deliverable (use nbconvert instead)
Solo projects where output preservation is desired in git
summary: nbstripout strips Jupyter notebook outputs before git commit. nbstripout --install installs git filter; auto-strips outputs on commit. .gitattributes: *.ipynb filter=nbstripout. Pre-commit hook integration. Strips output, execution_count. MIT. Per-repo install. Essential for clean notebook diffs and avoiding binary blobs in git. Use nbconvert if outputs are the deliverable.
